title: Last Factorial Digit
description:
The factorial of $N$, written as $N!$, is defined as the product of all the integers from $1$ to $N$. For example, $3! = 1 \times 2 \times 3 = 6$.

This number can be very large, so instead of computing the entire product, just compute the last digit of $N!$ (when $N!$ is written in base $10$).
inputDesc: The first line of input contains a positive integer $1 \leq T \leq 10$, the number of test cases. Each of the next $T$ lines contains a single positive integer $N$. $N$ is at most $10$.
outputDesc: For each value of $N$, print the last digit of $N!$.
